name: AJV (Another JSON Validator)
what_it_does: The fastest JSON Schema validator for JavaScript/TypeScript. Compiles JSON Schema definitions to optimized validation functions for maximum performance. Supports JSON Schema draft-04/06/07/2019-09/2020-12 and OpenAPI 3 schemas. Used by thousands of npm packages as a validation dependency. Core validator used by Fastify, TypeBox, and many API frameworks. Reference implementation for JSON Schema validation in JavaScript.
use_cases: Validate JSON data against JSON Schema definitions with maximum performance via compiled validation functions
Validate API request and response payloads in Fastify, Express, or other Node.js servers against OpenAPI schemas
Pre-compile validation schemas at startup and reuse compiled validators for high-throughput validation pipelines
Validate configuration files, data files, and user input against JSON Schema definitions
Generate TypeScript types from JSON Schema via AJV's addFormats and TypeScript integration
not_for: Teams wanting ergonomic TypeScript-first API — Zod or TypeBox offer better TypeScript DX than raw JSON Schema
Simple object validation without JSON Schema — Yup or Zod are more intuitive for application-level validation
Runtime TypeScript type generation — AJV validates JSON Schema; use TypeBox or json-schema-to-typescript for type generation
best_when: You have JSON Schema definitions (OpenAPI, JSON Schema files) and need maximum validation performance with a battle-tested, widely-used validator.
avoid_when: You prefer TypeScript-first schema definition — use Zod or TypeBox to define schemas in TypeScript and generate JSON Schema from them.

pricing.notes: MIT licensed. Completely free. Most downloaded JSON Schema validator in npm ecosystem.

performance.rate_limits: No rate limits — in-process compiled validation.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Validation errors are standard JSON Schema error objects with instancePath, schemaPath, and message fields. Error messages can be customized via ajv-errors plugin. Machine-readable error format.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Compiled validators are pure functions — same schema + same data = same validation result.
known_agent_gotchas: AJV v8 changed from v6 with breaking changes — ajv-formats, ajv-keywords are separate packages now; v6 schemas may need migration
Schema compilation is expensive — compile schemas once at startup and cache the validate functions for reuse
AJV strict mode (default in v8) rejects unknown keywords — schemas from older tools may need strict:false or keyword registration
additionalProperties is not set to false by default — extra object properties pass validation unless explicitly disallowed
Circular schema references ($ref) require careful setup — recursive schemas must be compiled in the correct order
Error messages are technical JSON Schema paths — use ajv-errors or a wrapper to produce user-friendly validation messages

security_score_components.security_notes: Validation library — no network calls. Compiled validation prevents prototype pollution and injection. MIT licensed. Most widely used JSON Schema validator in the ecosystem.
